fact: August 29 is exactly 241 days into the year, which means there are only 124 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is prime time for monarch butterflies to start their epic 3,000-mile journey south to Mexico - some of them are probably flying over your head right now!
The sun rises about 6 minutes later on August 29 than it did just two weeks earlier, and you can actually notice the difference if you pay attention!
Apple trees in many parts of North America are heavy with fruit right now, and some varieties are so ripe they're literally falling off the branches and bonking people on the head!
This date falls smack in the middle of Virgo season, which means anyone born today is supposedly super organized and loves making lists (whether that's true or not is up for debate)!
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere countries, August 29 is deep winter, and some places might even have snow on the ground while kids in America are sweating in summer heat!
Crickets are chirping like crazy around this time of year, and here's the weird part - you can actually tell the temperature by counting their chirps and doing some simple math!
The evening sky on August 29 gets dark about 30 minutes earlier than it did at the beginning of August, which means less time for outdoor games but more time for stargazing!
Many deciduous trees are secretly starting to shut down their green chlorophyll production right now, even though you can't see the fall colors yet - it's like nature's invisible countdown timer!

In northern regions, some animals like chipmunks and squirrels are in full panic mode, stuffing their cheeks with nuts and seeds because their bodies somehow know winter is coming in just a few months!
The Atlantic Ocean is at its warmest temperature of the entire year around this date, which is why late August is prime time for hurricanes to form and spin around like giant watery tornadoes!
August 29 is roughly 65 days away from the autumn equinox, which means we're officially in the countdown zone where each day has noticeably less sunlight than the one before!
